{"id":"mindspec-mol-zq3","title":"Implement 042-copilot-cli-support","description":"Implement the approved plan by working through beads.

**Bead cycle:**
1. `mindspec next --spec=042-copilot-cli-support` — claim next ready bead
2. Write code, tests, docs in the worktree
3. `mindspec complete --spec=042-copilot-cli-support` — close bead, advance state

Repeat until all beads are closed. `mindspec complete` transitions to review
mode when the last bead is done.

Run `mindspec instruct --spec=042-copilot-cli-support` for implementation guidance.

{"id":"mindspec-mol-7d0","title":"spec-lifecycle","description":"MindSpec spec lifecycle: spec → approve → plan → approve → implement → review.

This formula tracks the full lifecycle of a single specification from drafting
through implementation and final review. Human gates ensure quality checkpoints
between phases.

## Usage

Create a molecule for a new spec:
```bash
bd mol wisp create spec-lifecycle --var spec_id=039-my-feature
```

Or for an existing spec that's already in progress:
```bash
bd mol wisp create spec-lifecycle --var spec_id=038-beads-native-multi-spec-state
```

## Artifacts Produced

Each phase creates or updates files under `.mindspec/docs/specs/046-worktree-enforcement/`:
- `spec.md` — the specification (Phase 1)
- `context-pack.md` — generated context bundle (Phase 2, on approval)
- `plan.md` — the implementation plan (Phase 3)
- `proofs/` — optional proof outputs (Phase 5)

{"id":"mindspec-d6t8","title":"ApprovePlan should auto-create implementation beads from plan sections","description":"ApprovePlan validates and approves the plan but does not create implementation beads. Today bead creation is entirely manual — the agent must run bd create for each bead, set parents, and wire dependencies by hand. This is error-prone (as seen in 047 where beads were created without parents).\\n\\nThe fix: add a step to ApprovePlan that parses plan.md for ## Bead N: ... sections, creates child beads under the implement molecule via bd create --parent, wires inter-bead dependencies from the Depends on lines, and writes generated bead IDs back into plan.md frontmatter.\\n\\nRelevant code:\\n- internal/approve/plan.go (ApprovePlan)\\n- internal/validate/plan.go (already parses bead sections for validation)\\n- cmd/mindspec/approve.go (approve plan subcommand)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"max@enubiq.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T12:06:14Z","created_by":"Max Steel","updated_at":"2026-02-26T12:06:14Z"}

{"id":"mindspec-mol-jpkyf","title":"spec-lifecycle","description":"MindSpec spec lifecycle: spec → approve → plan → approve → implement → review.

This formula tracks the full lifecycle of a single specification from drafting
through implementation and final review. Human gates ensure quality checkpoints
between phases.

## Usage

Create a molecule for a new spec:
```bash
bd mol wisp create spec-lifecycle --var spec_id=039-my-feature
```

Or for an existing spec that's already in progress:
```bash
bd mol wisp create spec-lifecycle --var spec_id=038-beads-native-multi-spec-state
```

## Artifacts Produced

Each phase creates or updates files under `.mindspec/docs/specs/053-drop-state-json/`:
- `spec.md` — the specification (Phase 1)
- `context-pack.md` — generated context bundle (Phase 2, on approval)
- `plan.md` — the implementation plan (Phase 3)
- `proofs/` — optional proof outputs (Phase 5)

{"id":"mindspec-mol-07lst","title":"Implement 053-drop-state-json","description":"Implement the approved plan by working through beads.

**Bead cycle:**
1. `mindspec next --spec=053-drop-state-json` — claim next ready bead
2. Write code, tests, docs in the worktree
3. `mindspec complete --spec=053-drop-state-json` — close bead, advance state

Repeat until all beads are closed. `mindspec complete` transitions to review
mode when the last bead is done.

Run `mindspec instruct --spec=053-drop-state-json` for implementation guidance.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T08:10:28Z","updated_at":"2026-02-27T08:10:28Z"}

old_value: {"id":"mindspec-t3wc","title":"FindRoot() returns worktree root instead of main repo root","description":"When running mindspec commands from inside a worktree that has .mindspec/, FindRoot() stops at the worktree root instead of resolving to the main repo. This causes spec-init to create nested worktrees (.worktrees/wt-054/.worktrees/wt-033) and writes a doubled activeWorktree path to the focus file that cannot be corrected via state set. Git worktrees have a .git file (not directory) pointing back to the main repo — FindRoot() should detect this and continue walking up, or resolve via git rev-parse --show-toplevel.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"max@enubiq.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T08:39:02Z","created_by":"Max Steel","updated_at":"2026-02-28T08:39:02Z"}

old_value: {"id":"mindspec-wpqg","title":"Focus file deadlock: cannot change active spec when workflow guard and worktree-bash hook are both active","description":"When multiple specs are active and the current focus is in plan/spec mode, the agent hits a deadlock: (1) Edit tool on .mindspec/focus is blocked by workflow guard (plan mode blocks code edits, and focus is treated as code), (2) mindspec state set via Bash is blocked by worktree-bash hook (CWD is main, not the active worktree). The agent cannot change the active spec or mode without hopping through an allowed worktree to write the focus file — a fragile workaround. Fix options: (a) whitelist .mindspec/focus in the workflow guard, (b) whitelist mindspec state commands in the worktree-bash hook allowlist, (c) both.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"richardmaxsteel@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T09:03:15Z","created_by":"mrmaxsteel","updated_at":"2026-03-01T09:03:15Z"}

old_value: {"id":"mindspec-xhdb","title":"ActiveSpecs fails to discover spec from nested bead worktree","description":"When running mindspec complete from a bead worktree (root/.worktrees/worktree-spec-X/.worktrees/worktree-beadID/), ActiveSpecs scans root/.worktrees/worktree-spec-* and finds the spec worktree. But the lifecycle.yaml is in the spec worktree's .mindspec/docs/specs/\u003cid\u003e/ directory. SpecDir(root, specID) checks root/.worktrees/worktree-spec-\u003cid\u003e/.mindspec/docs/specs/\u003cid\u003e/ which should work. The bug may be that ActiveSpecs returns the spec but the complete command's ResolveTarget fails due to multiple active specs from OTHER specs (044, 056). Need to investigate whether the actual bug is in ResolveTarget not accepting focus-based targeting, or in ActiveSpecs not seeing the right specs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"richardmaxsteel@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T10:56:00Z","created_by":"mrmaxsteel","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:56:00Z"}

new_value: Focus now checked before ActiveSpecs in ResolveTarget — no more false ambiguity errors

old_value: {"id":"mindspec-0fm7","title":"Focus file points to deleted worktree, blocks all tool operations","description":"When a bead worktree is cleaned up but focus file isn't updated, the worktree-bash and worktree-file hooks block ALL tool operations because they redirect to the stale path. This is a chicken-and-egg problem: you can't fix the focus file because the hooks read it and block edits. Needs: 1) mindspec complete should update focus on cleanup, 2) hooks should gracefully handle missing worktree paths instead of hard-blocking.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"richardmaxsteel@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T19:08:05Z","created_by":"mrmaxsteel","updated_at":"2026-02-28T23:29:15Z"}

new_value: Fixed: hooks validate ActiveWorktree exists on disk (breaks deadlock), cleanup.go clears focus after worktree removal (prevents stale state)
Obsolete: fixed in latest beads version

old_value: {"id":"mindspec-trb4","title":"Workflow guard blocks code edits for non-spec infrastructure when any spec is in plan mode","description":"When multiple specs are active and one is in plan mode, the workflow guard blocks ALL code edits — even for work unrelated to the plan-mode spec. The guard reads the focus file mode without considering whether the edit is in a different spec's worktree or is shared infrastructure. This forced a focus file switch just to edit test harness files during spec-044 planning.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"richardmaxsteel@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T09:01:52Z","created_by":"mrmaxsteel","updated_at":"2026-03-01T09:01:52Z"}

old_value: {"id":"mindspec-cgp4","title":"[workflow] Fix explore dismiss panic without prompt workarounds","description":"Scope: fix the crash path behind 'mindspec explore dismiss' observed in TestLLM_AbandonSpec.\\n\\nRequirements:\\n- Root-cause and fix nil dereference/panic in explore dismiss flow.\\n- Keep the test prompt task-oriented; do NOT patch by adding scripted command hints to scenario prompts.\\n- Add deterministic unit/integration coverage for dismiss command success + failure modes.\\n\\nAcceptance criteria:\\n- 'mindspec explore dismiss' exits 0 in expected happy-path state.\\n- env -u CLAUDECODE go test ./internal/harness -v -run TestLLM_AbandonSpec -timeout 10m -count=1 passes.\\n- No new tautological prompt instructions introduced in scenario prompts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"richardmaxsteel@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T09:02:39Z","created_by":"mrmaxsteel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T09:02:39Z"}

new_value: Fixed nil-focus panic in explore dismiss/promote; added nil-state coverage; TestLLM_AbandonSpec passes

old_value: {"id":"mindspec-i0io","title":"[workflow] Fix approve-impl review-\u003eidle focus persistence","description":"Scope: fix state transition persistence where 'mindspec approve impl' succeeds but root focus remains review (TestLLM_ImplApprove).\\n\\nRequirements:\\n- Audit and correct focus write target/root behavior across repo root vs spec worktree contexts.\\n- Ensure post-approve state is consistently idle from assertion roots used by harness.\\n- Add deterministic tests around approve-impl state writes.\\n\\nAcceptance criteria:\\n- env -u CLAUDECODE go test ./internal/harness -v -run TestLLM_ImplApprove -timeout 10m -count=1 passes.\\n- Added deterministic test fails pre-fix and passes post-fix.\\n- Fix is in workflow/state logic, not by weakening assertions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"richardmaxsteel@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T09:02:39Z","created_by":"mrmaxsteel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T09:02:39Z"}

new_value: Fixed approve impl focus persistence: fallback to root focus when local missing, write idle focus to local+root, added deterministic tests, TestLLM_ImplApprove + SingleBead regression pass

old_value: {"id":"mindspec-ce5b","title":"[workflow] Enforce deterministic spec/bead branch+worktree cleanup","description":"Scope: fix cleanup leakage after lifecycle completion (SpecToIdle still leaves spec/*, bead/*, and worktrees).\\n\\nRequirements:\\n- Harden cleanup orchestration for merged/closed lifecycle: remove bead branches, spec branch, linked worktrees (including nested cases) deterministically.\\n- Preserve valid safety guards while eliminating cleanup leftovers.\\n- Add deterministic coverage for cleanup behavior in lifecycle completion path.\\n\\nAcceptance criteria:\\n- env -u CLAUDECODE go test ./internal/harness -v -run TestLLM_SpecToIdle -timeout 15m -count=1 passes cleanup assertions.\\n- env -u CLAUDECODE go test ./internal/harness -v -run TestLLM_CompleteFromSpecWorktree -timeout 10m -count=1 remains green.\\n- No prompt-level tautological hacks added.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"richardmaxsteel@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T09:02:39Z","created_by":"mrmaxsteel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T09:02:39Z"}

{"id":"mindspec-n9j7","title":"[instructions] Strengthen non-tautological implement workflow guidance","description":"Scope: improve MindSpec instructions/guidance so agents follow intended workflow in complex implement scenarios without embedding workflow scripts in prompts.\\n\\nPrimary targets:\\n- TestLLM_MultiBeadDeps (must use next/worktrees correctly).\\n- TestLLM_InterruptForBug (must still produce required feature artifact).\\n\\nRequirements:\\n- Adjust shared guidance surfaces (instruct templates/CLI messaging/hooks) only.\\n- Keep scenario prompts task-oriented and non-tautological.\\n- Add/adjust deterministic checks where needed to guard regressions.\\n\\nAcceptance criteria:\\n- env -u CLAUDECODE go test ./internal/harness -v -run 'TestLLM_MultiBeadDeps|TestLLM_InterruptForBug' -timeout 20m -count=1 passes.\\n- env -u CLAUDECODE go test ./internal/harness -v -run TestLLM_SingleBead -timeout 10m -count=1 remains green.\\n- TESTING.md updated with metric deltas and rationale.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"richardmaxsteel@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T09:02:39Z","created_by":"mrmaxsteel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T09:02:39Z"}

{"id":"mindspec-kt01","title":"[harness] Final full-suite verification and reporting","description":"Scope: once issues 1-4 are complete, run full harness validation and document outcomes.\\n\\nRequirements:\\n- Preflight: make build; bd dolt killall.\\n- Run full suite: env -u CLAUDECODE go test ./internal/harness/ -v -run '^TestLLM_' -timeout 180m -count=1.\\n- Update internal/harness/TESTING.md with new rows + session summary + known issue status changes.\\n\\nAcceptance criteria:\\n- Full-suite result and per-scenario metrics recorded with concrete numbers (events, turns, time).\\n- Documentation reflects what changed in workflow/instructions vs runtime logic.\\n- Session close protocol done (commit/push).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"richardmaxsteel@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T09:02:40Z","created_by":"mrmaxsteel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T09:02:40Z"}
